title: Add regression tests for ordering and state-transition invariants
summary: Add focused regression tests that lock in canonical transaction ordering and state-transition behavior so invalid begin/verify/file-intent/state combinations fail clearly while valid flows continue to pass.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover task lifecycle prerequisites, including rejection of task lifecycle events before tx.begin.
Tests cover verify ordering invariants, including rejection of tx.verify.pass and tx.verify.fail before tx.verify.start.
Tests cover file intent ordering invariants, including rejection of tx.file_intent.update to state=verified before tx.verify.pass and rejection of updates for missing file intents.
Tests cover transaction state validation invariants, including invalid verify_state status values and invalid phase/status combinations.
Tests assert clear, stable error messages for ordering and state-transition violations.
Tests confirm at least one valid canonical flow still succeeds end-to-end after the regression coverage is added.

steps.description: Identify existing tests and helper utilities that exercise transaction ordering, file intent transitions, and tx_state validation.
Add regression tests for begin/task lifecycle prerequisites and verify.start before verify result invariants.
Add regression tests for file_intent verified transitions, missing file intent updates, and commit gating prerequisites where applicable.
Add regression tests for invalid state snapshots such as unsupported verify_state statuses and phase/status mismatches.
Add a positive-path regression test proving the canonical event-first transaction flow still succeeds.

notes: Keep these tests narrow and deterministic so failures point directly to ordering or state invariant regressions.
Prefer explicit assertions on error strings mentioned in the updated draft where behavior is intended to remain user-visible.
Avoid duplicating empty-log initialization coverage already owned by p4-t1 except where it is required to reach a state-ordering assertion.
